title: trifolium pratense f. sanguineum
intro: Trifolium pratense f. sanguineum, also known as Red Clover, is a subspecies of the red clover plant, a legume with crimson flowers that grows in temperate climates. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its rich content of isoflavones, particularly formononetin and genistein, which are believed to have estrogen-like properties and offer potential health benefits. The medicinal properties of Red Clover include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of menopause, hot flashes, and osteoporosis, as well as support heart health and immune function. Red Clover is typically prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and its herbal preparations have been shown to promote overall well-being, improve cognitive function, and reduce the risk of certain cancers.
